run_summary: Portfolio sitting with 4 open positions, all in green to modest gains. Four new flags today are re-discoveries (NTLA, PTCT, ALGN already held) plus fresh entries (JBLU, AEIS, VECO, AMR, AVAV). AVAV carries a re-entry warning from a loss 17 days ago on identical geopolitical thesis—conf 3 UNDERDONE now fails the conf-4 re-entry bar. My track record shows 57% hit rate at conf 3 overall, 60% at conf 4, with OVERDONE calls lagging at 58% vs UNDERDONE at 64%. Existing positions remain intact on their original catalysts; new names warrant selective entry.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker traffic resumption pushing oil lower; JBLU as highest fuel-sensitivity carrier
entries.thesis: Double-digit move on a small-cap airline with no 8-K likely tracks the Strait of Hormuz reopening and the resulting oil weakness (XLE -1.65%) — JBLU is the highest-beta US carrier on jet fuel, so it amplifies any oil-down narrative. But moves of this magnitude on macro alone, in a structurally challenged carrier with weak balance sheet, tend to fade once the energy narrative stabilizes. Bear-side bias on fade.
entries.what_kills: An actual JBLU-specific catalyst emerges (merger talk, route deal, guidance raise) — would reframe as fundamental rather than macro sympathy
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged on +10.72% move and +14.06% five-day gain, driven by Strait of Hormuz oil-supply story lifting JBLU as high-beta airline carrier. Setup is transparently sector/macro sympathy (XLE -1.65%) with no company-specific catalyst—thesis expects fade within 2-3 sessions. My OVERDONE hit rate is 58% (below average), and this setup is textbook momentum unwind on macro normalization rather than a durable fundamental re-rating. Position sizing would be 15-25% conviction or 6% exploratory. Neither tier justifies entry on a fade thesis in a structurally weak carrier. Wait for reversal signal (oil bounces and JBLU gives back >50% of move) before reconsidering.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Semi rally; possible residual effect of 6/12 8-K
entries.thesis: AEIS is power conversion for semi-cap equipment — direct beneficiary of the chip rally. The 8-K from June 12 had only 8.01 + 9.01 items (catch-all + boilerplate), which are not severity indicators on their own. Move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement (we can't read the URL contents from here), the move could be idiosyncratic and have more legs
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 flag on +5.45% move into a supportive semi-equipment sector rally (SMH strength). Setup notes benign 8-K from 6/12 with only 8.01+9.01 items—'catch-all + boilerplate' per analyst, not severity indicators. Thesis is pure sector beta, not idiosyncratic catalyst. Analyst explicitly notes the 8-K *could* hide guidance pre-announcement (which would reframe move), but without reading the exhibit I cannot confirm that. This is 'wait for confirmation' territory, not buy. If the 8-K genuinely contained material guidance, price would likely be higher already and risk/reward skewed. WATCH and revisit if semi rally extends and AEIS breaks out on follow-on news.

entries.name: Align Technology
entries.catalyst: Apparent positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on; no specific filing
entries.thesis: ALGN up ~5% on a day XLV is down 0.87% is a notable relative-strength signal — the name is going up against its sector tape, with elevated volume (1.76x). No 8-K and no earnings, but the combination of risk-on + IWM strength + consumer discretionary +1.45% suggests ALGN is being re-rated as a discretionary recovery name. If risk-on persists, a beaten-down consumer-medtech can keep grinding higher as shorts cover.
entries.what_kills: Reversal of risk-on tape; XLV continues to bleed and ALGN follows; an 8-K with adverse content appears
entries.reasoning: ALGN is already held from today's open. This new flag re-surfaces the same name with UNDERDONE conf 3, citing continued counter-tape relative strength and risk-on positioning shift. I've already sized ALGN at 3.47 shares (~1.8% of bankroll), well under 25% position limit. Thesis status on open position is 'intact' and I'm choosing HOLD. The new flag is not triggering an ADD because: (1) original entry was exploratory-tier strength (positioning signal + counter-tape move + elevated volume) and I'm already capturing that view; (2) days held is 0, so position has not matured; (3) no NEW information vs. the original flag has emerged yet (no upgrade, no XLV reversal, no consumer-discretionary peer guide). WATCH for confirmation (analyst upgrade, consumer peers printing firm, XLV reversal) and consider ADD only if thesis visibly strengthens.

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
entries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
entries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
entries.reasoning: NTLA is already held from entry 3 days ago. New flag re-surfaces same name with RATIONAL conf 3, citing UniQure FDA-pathway news + NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01) as coherent regulatory re-rating. I'm already holding and thesis is intact (position +22.38% unrealized, XBI +3% group strength). No new information has emerged that would justify an ADD: the UniQure news was already the setup when I entered; NTLA's exhibit has not been read and no new interim data or partnership has been announced since. WATCH for any follow-on XBI momentum or NTLA-specific filing that would strengthen conviction, but current hold position is adequate. Adding now would be chasing extended move without fresh catalyst.

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
entries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
entries.reasoning: PTCT is already held from entry 1 day ago. New flag re-surfaces same name with RATIONAL conf 3, citing 6/16 8-K (8.01+9.01, likely regulatory or partnership) into strong XBI tape. Position unrealized +4.36% on 1.98x volume. I've already entered at exploratory-like size (8.0 shares, ~0.8% of bankroll) to test the 8-K thesis. No new information since entry: exhibit has not been confirmed, no follow-on filing has dropped, XBI is still strong but not accelerating. WATCH for exhibit clarity (next 24-48h via 8-K detail or analyst notes) and consider ADD only if the exhibit reveals a material regulatory milestone or partnership that strengthens conviction. Current hold captures the setup adequately.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
entries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged on -9.44% one-day drop after +11.92% five-day rally on sector beta (no company catalyst). Setup is textbook crowded-trade unwind: small-cap semi-equipment with low float ran hard in sympathy with ONTO/AEIS, now unwinding harder as SMH stabilizes. Thesis explicitly flags this as 'fade if sector base holds.' But VECO is already down hard today (one-day -9.44%) which means much of the unwinding is priced. Fade thesis would favor a *short* or cash hold, not a long entry. My OVERDONE hit rate is 58% and this particular setup (oversold unwind in a crowded mini-momentum cohort) is historically prone to whipsaw—not enough edge to justify entry. SKIP and monitor: if VECO stabilizes above today's lows and SMH continues to bounce, may revisit as WATCH.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XLE / oil weakness on US-Iran deal framework
entries.thesis: AMR -5.24% today and -5.89% over 5 days as XLE -3.48% on the US-Iran deal — but AMR is coking coal for steel, not thermal coal or oil. The fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz oil supply is weak; met coal pricing tracks Chinese steel demand and Australian export tonnages, not Brent. Below-average volume (0.77x) on the selloff suggests passive ETF/energy-complex outflows rather than dedicated fundamental selling. Setup for a relative-value bounce if energy stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: Hard coking coal benchmark prices roll over; Chinese steel demand prints weak; AMR-specific operational issue surfaces
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged on -5.24% one-day and -5.89% five-day move on XLE sympathy (energy-complex outflows). Setup notes below-1x volume (0.77x) on the selloff, indicating passive ETF/energy-sector mechanics rather than fundamental met-coal weakness. Thesis: AMR is coking coal (steel demand linked), not thermal coal / oil—fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz is weak. This is a classic relative-value setup: AMR should *bounce* if energy stabilizes, not be bought into the dip. Buying into a energy-sympathy selloff on below-average volume is a fade trade, not a conviction conviction upside bet. My OVERDONE hit rate is 58% and this thesis expects reversal timing within weeks—unclear catalyst for bounce timing. SKIP. WATCH for energy stabilization + AMR relative bounce, then consider entry.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran deal-signing headline + broader defense weakness (ITA -0.95% on risk-on day)
entries.thesis: AVAV's drone-heavy revenue mix is disproportionately exposed to active conflict demand. With Trump admin signaling Iran deal signing 'in coming days' and ITA underperforming, the de-rating could extend if a deal actually prints. No 8-K signal so this is sector/macro driven, not company-specific — but the divergence vs. a risk-on tape is notable.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal falls through (Trump-admin source said 'not 100% certain'); AVAV announces a new DoD contract; broader defense bounces as buyers treat dip as opportunity
entries.reasoning: AVAV carries a re-entry warning: closed 17 days ago for a -5.31% loss on identical OVERDONE geopolitical thesis (Iran missile / defense sector bid). New flag is UNDERDONE conf 3 on -7.14% one-day / -8.25% five-day move in sympathy with Iran deal-signing headline (de-escalation, ITA weakness, defense unwind). Re-entry discipline requires (1) confidence >= 4 and (2) genuinely new information not known at exit. This flag is conf 3 (fails bar 1), and the information is continuation of the *same* geopolitical Iran narrative I already closed on—Trump admin said deal will sign 'in coming days' then, and is saying 'in coming days' now (fails bar 2, no new information). My exit reasoning was explicit: 'geopolitical thesis has NOT evolved, move has overextended, fade expected within two weeks.' I see no evidence thesis has evolved or that new facts have emerged. WATCH and only revisit if (a) a *signed* deal actually prints (new fact) AND (b) AVAV-specific catalyst (new DoD contract, partnership) surfaces to justify reframing as fundamental rather than macro.

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news, no earnings proximity flagged
entries.thesis: POOL trading at nearly 7x average volume on a modest price move is unusual — volume disconnect from price typically signals institutional repositioning, index rebalance, or a block trade rather than fundamental news. No 8-K, no earnings window flagged. Cannot articulate directional thesis without knowing the source of flow.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be a single block cross with no follow-through tomorrow

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.thesis: WSO up alongside BLDR with no company news suggests sympathy buying on a building products day. Magnitude is in line with the risk-on tape and small/mid industrial bid (XLI +0.73%, IWM +2%). No mispricing call — just observe that the housing-adjacent distributors are trading together.
entries.what_kills: WSO-specific 8-K appears showing M&A or guide cut/raise

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semiconductor sector rally led by TSM
entries.thesis: ONTO move is fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%. Semicap equipment names (VECO, AEIS, ONTO) all up similarly — beta to the leader, magnitude appropriate. No idiosyncratic catalyst, no mispricing.
entries.what_kills: Onto-specific guidance or order news appears — would reclassify as fundamental

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing dragging pharma sector
entries.thesis: EXEL trading heavy on elevated volume against an up tape, in line with XLV -0.87% weakness. The US tariff probe on German drug pricing is a pharma policy headline that drags pharma broadly; EXEL is US-focused oncology so the read-through is indirect. Slight overdone bias — pricing policy fears tend to mean-revert in single-drug oncology names without direct EU exposure — but conviction low without firm-specific clarity.
entries.what_kills: An EXEL-specific negative readout, FDA action, or competitive trial result emerges — would justify the weakness fundamentally

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: Beam has run hard over 5 days into today's continuation. XBI is only +0.95%, so this is outperforming the biotech tape. Could be conference data anticipation, M&A speculation, or a pipeline readout — cannot identify which from available evidence. Genuinely cannot articulate either side at conf 3+.

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
entries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
entries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
entries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
entries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
entries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.
